description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular barrier constructed from a series of large, irregularly shaped dark stone blocks. The stones are stacked in two uneven rows, with each block exhibiting natural, jagged edges and a coarse, weathered surface. The coloration is predominantly dark brown to black, with subtle variations and mottling that suggest exposure to the elements. The gaps between the stones are irregular, and there is no visible mortar, indicating a dry-stacked construction. The overall structure is solid and heavy, with a rugged, utilitarian appearance typical of traditional stonework. This type of barrier would be effective at blocking passage or demarcating boundaries in an outdoor urban or park setting.
description: A rugged, dry-stacked barrier made of large, dark, irregular stone blocks with a coarse, weathered surface. The stones are tightly packed in two uneven rows, forming a solid and imposing obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the stone barrier, with two rows of uneven, jagged blocks and visible gaps between stones.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth and thickness of the stacked stones, highlighting the irregular profiles and layered arrangement.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the back faces of the stone blocks and their rough, natural surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thickness and the irregular, protruding shapes of the stones.
attribute_location_context: urban park or garden landscaping barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: The stones show signs of surface weathering with mottled coloration and some chipped edges, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall
